mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-hike periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals an impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning by institutional investors and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate spikes by more than 0.5 percentage points within three months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive monetary policy compress real disposable income and corporate profit margins, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a supply-constrained macroeconomic environment indicates successful pent-up demand release, which directly boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward equity returns are not significantly higher during industrial production upswells compared to downswells over a 12-month backtest, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC consistently.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in housing starts reflects weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, which precedes broader economic slowdown and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1000 for two consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal exhibits no predictive power when controlling for concurrent changes in the federal funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Robust payroll growth coupled with a stable unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor market signal fails to generate positive forward equity returns in more than 50% of rolling 3-year windows, or the marginal effect of payroll growth vanishes when unemployment is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in retail sales demonstrates resilient consumer demand, which directly supports S&P 500 earnings revisions and produces positive forward equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of periods where retail sales show consecutive monthly gains, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation forces prolonged monetary tightening, which increases the cost of capital and compresses equity multiples, systematically driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain stable in more than 45% of high-inflation episodes despite rate hikes, or the interaction between inflation and policy rates shows no statistically significant effect on forward returns.
